title: Vulcan Materials Company (VMC) Q4 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Vulcan Materials Company (NYSE:VMC ) Q4 2022 Earnings Conference Call February 16, 2023 11:00 AM ET Company Participants Mark Warren - VP of IR Thomas Hill - Chairman, President and CEO Mary Andrews Carlisle - SVP and CFO Conference Call Participants Stanley Elliott - Stifel Trey Grooms - Stephens Inc. Kathryn Thompson - Thompson Research Group Anthony Pettinari - Citi Jerry Revich - Goldman Sachs Michael Feniger - Bank of America Mike Dahl - RBC Capital Markets Tyler Brown - Raymond James Phil Ng - Jefferies Garik Shmois - Loop Capital Keith Hughes - Truist Michael Dudas - Vertical Research Adam Thalhimer - Thompson Davis Rohit Seth - Seaport Research Partners Operator Good morning, ladies and gentlemen, and welcome to Vulcan Materials Company's Fourth Quarter Earnings Call. My name is Gretchen, and I will be your conference call coordinator today.

title: Vulcan (VMC) Q4 Earnings Miss, Adjusted EBITDA Down, Stock Fall
text: Vulcan's (VMC) fourth-quarter results benefit from higher pricing. Yet, lower volumes and weather woes offset the positives.

title: Vulcan Materials (VMC) Q4 Earnings and Revenues Lag Estimates
text: Vulcan (VMC) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -16.28% and 4.81%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Vulcan Materials (VMC) to Post Q4 Earnings: What to Expect?
text: Non-residential and infrastructural activities are likely to have aided Vulcan Materials' (VMC) Q4 results. Inflationary pressure, wet weather and a slowdown in the residential market may have been risks.

title: Vulcan (VMC) Rewards Investors With a 7.5% Dividend Increase
text: Vulcan (VMC) hikes quarterly dividend by 7.5% to 43 cents per share.

title: Vulcan Materials: No Fundamental Basis For The High Price
text: Vulcan Materials is mostly a construction aggregates Group. The US aggregates demand (tonnage) is cyclical which makes VMC a cyclical company. Notwithstanding its good results over the past few years, VMC's performance over the cycle is poor. There was no shipment tonnage growth. Revenue growth was due to price increases.
